pathAssistantSteps.info:
• Specify the Reason - Select the most accurate reason for losing the deal (e.g., competitor pricing, customer budget, internal delay, or requirement change). This helps track loss patterns and improve strategy.
  • Update Main Competitor (if applicable) - Fill in this field only when the opportunity is lost due to a competitor. Identifying which competitor won supports better market analysis and sales improvement.
  • Capture Customer Feedback - Record customer input on why the deal was not finalized to refine offerings and negotiation approaches.
  • Update Final Details - Ensure the opportunity record is complete with all communications, quotes, and closing notes.
  • Review for Future Follow-up -Note if the customer may re-engage later and plan accordingly.

• Verify Final Details – Ensure all opportunity information such as product, pricing, and quantity matches the approved quote.
  • Confirm Order Completion – Check that the synced order and related documents are accurate and approved for processing.
  • Update Payment and Delivery Info – Record payment confirmation, delivery schedule, or handover details to operations.
  • Notify Internal Teams – Inform fulfillment, finance, and support teams to initiate post-sales processes.
  • Capture Customer Feedback – Document positive inputs or improvement suggestions for future reference.
  • Celebrate and Close Properly – Mark the opportunity as Closed Won, ensuring all related records (quotes, orders, activities) are complete and consistent.

• Plan Transport And Schedule: Confirm transport dates, routes, and delivery mode based on the customer’s preferred timeline.
  • Coordinate with Logistics Team: Ensure dispatch readiness and resource allocation for timely delivery.
  • Verify Delivery Address & Contact: Double-check customer delivery details to avoid shipment errors.
  • Track Delivery Progress: Monitor the shipment status and communicate updates to the customer proactively.
  • Document Completion: Record delivery confirmation and update any proof of delivery for tracking and audit purposes.

• Select Document Types: Choose the correct document category (e.g., Financial, Address Proof, ID Proof) as required.
  • Attach Required Documents: Upload all necessary files in the Documents Attached field before submission.
  • Update Verification Status: Set the status to Submitted, In Review, Verified, or Rejected based on progress.
  • Record Verification Date: Enter the completion date for accurate audit tracking.
  • Provide Feedback: Add remarks in Document Verification Feedback for missing or incorrect details.

• Initiate Sales Agreement: Enable this option to create a Sales Agreement. It will be generated using the synced Order details from this Opportunity.
  • Generate Invoice: Create the invoice with accurate order details, taxes, and payment terms.
  • Verify Accuracy: Double-check invoice number, customer details, and billed amounts before sending.
  • Share with Customer: Send the invoice promptly and confirm receipt.
  • Track Payment Status: Monitor payments against due dates and update status accordingly.
  • Follow Up: Communicate proactively for pending or delayed payments and document all interactions.

• Review Quote Status: Confirm the quote has final pricing, product details, and approved discounts before sharing.
  • Confirm Payment Terms: Select the correct payment option based on discussions and approvals, ensuring mutual agreement on schedules.
  • Assess Purchase Timeline: Identify when the customer plans to buy for accurate forecasting and follow-ups.
  • Address Objections: Resolve pricing, delivery, or product concerns promptly to maintain trust.
  • Obtain Approvals: Secure internal approvals for revised terms or discounts and document all negotiation details.

• Specify the Reason - Select or mention why the opportunity is on hold (e.g., budget issues, internal delays, or customer decision pending) to track and analyze delay trends.
  • Set Expected Resume Date - Enter a realistic date for resuming progress to maintain pipeline visibility and timely follow-ups.
  • Communicate with Customer - Keep the customer updated on status and next steps to maintain engagement and trust.
  • Review Internal Dependencies - Identify and resolve any internal blockers or pending approvals causing the delay.
  • Monitor Regularly - Revisit on-hold deals to decide whether to resume, close, or requalify based on updated info.
  • Update Main Competitor - Complete this field only if the deal is on hold due to competitor involvement.

• Sync the Order: Sync the order with the opportunity after customer approvals and internal checks to keep systems consistent.
  • Verify Order Number: Confirm the Order Number is auto-generated or correctly entered for accurate tracking.
  • Cross-check Order Details: Validate quantities, pricing, and discounts before finalizing the synced order.
  • Coordinate with Operations: Share confirmed order details with fulfillment/delivery teams for timely execution.
  • Maintain Documentation: Keep records of the synced order and related documents for compliance and audits.

• Confirm Product Family And SubCategory: Ensure the selected product family and Subcategory aligns with the customer’s business needs.
  • Verify Dealer Acknowledgement: Confirm the dealer has reviewed and acknowledged the opportunity.
  • Document Key Requirements/Notes: Record the customer’s main needs, preferences, and expectations for accurate quoting.
  • Ensure Primary Contact Added and Validated: Add and verify the main customer contact for effective communication and follow-ups.

• Select Appropriate Price Book: Choose the correct price book based on customer type, region, or product category to prevent pricing errors.
  • Create and Sync Quote: Generate a quote with accurate products, quantities, and pricing, then sync the most relevant quote with the opportunity.
  • Apply Discount (if applicable): Apply discounts per company policy or approval limits, ensuring they’re justified and documented.
  • Verify Quote Accuracy: Review product configurations, taxes, and terms before sending the quote to the customer.
  • Communicate Clearly with Customer: Share the quote promptly and confirm the customer’s understanding of pricing, inclusions, and validity.
